content.full_text:
Keith J. Schneider
Executive Vice President
Building Industry Assn. of
Central California, Inc.
1401 F Street
Modesto, CA 95354

Re: FPPC Advice No. A-86-110

Dear Mr. Schneider:

This is in response to your letter dated March 12, 1986,
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000 - 91015).
Specifically, you asked whether the Builders, Associates,
Contractors Political Action Committee (BAC-PAC) must file an
amended Statement of Organization to comply with the new
requirements for identification of committee sponsors.

Beginning January 1, 1986, sponsored recipient campaign
committees were required to include the names of their sponsors
in the names of the committees (Gov. Code Section 84106).
Because, as you have stated, the BAC-PAC is sponsored by the
Building Industry Association of Central California, the PAC is
required to amend its statement of Organization to include the
name of the Association in the committee's name. One suggestion
would be to simply call it the Building Industry Association of
Central California Political Action Committee. Please note that
you may abbreviate some of the standard, publicly recognized
words in the name, such as Assn. for Association, CA for
California, and PAC for Political Action Committee. If desired,
you may also retain the committee's old acronym as part of its
new name, i.e., Building Industry Assn. of Central CA BAC-PAC,
or Building Industry Assn. of Central CA PAC (BAC-PAC).

Finally, you should also be aware that a sponsored
committee may have only one name and that name must be used
whenever the committee is required by law to be identified.
This includes the committee's Statement of Organization (Form
410), all of its campaign disclosure statements, all of its mass
mailings, and the campaign disclosure statements filed by
candidates and committees which receive a contribution of $100
or more from the committee. Therefore, I suggest that the
committee's new name also be printed on its stationery and
checks.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Enclosed is a copy of the 1986 Form 410 which you should
use to amend the name of the committee. Please contact me at
(916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

[illegible signature]
Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

CW:cab
Enclosures

PHONE 529-4531                                                MODESTO, CA 95354


March 12, 1986


California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA.  95804-0807

Dear Sir:

I've just received your FPPC Fact Sheet C-1, dated November,
1985 concerning new name identification requirements
for sponsored committees.  It wasn't real clear whether
we needed to change our PAC name or not.  Our current
name is Builders, Associates, Contractors Political
Action Committee (BAC-PAC), sponsored by the Building
Industry Association of Central California (BIACC).
Is a name change necessary?  If so, what would you suggest?

Thank you for your advice.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Keith J. Schneider
Executive Vice President

KJS:mb
cc: Russ Harrison, Chairman
    BAC-PAC




                                                            Affiliated with

                                            NATIONAL ASSOCIATION      CALIFORNIA BUILDING
Formerly Home Builders Assn:                OF HOME BUILDERS          INDUSTRY ASSOC.

[illegible]

HCR 4 III 40 AM 06

1355 E. 10th Street
Chico, CA 95928

April 2, 1986


FPPC
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear FPPC,

I have a few simple questions!

In your 1985 manual (A Guide for Candidates and Treasurers) it is
stated on page 15 under B.(1):

     "Candidates" are not required to file a statement of
     Organization unless they choose to create a committee.

Since a "Committee" must file such a statement even if they have
only 1 member (i.e., treasurer) what's the difference between a
1-person member committee and a candidate who doesn't choose to
have a "committee"?

The other question is, if the candidate chooses not to file a Form
410, then I assume they never will have an ID issued by the Secre-
tary of State?

The third question is: If they never filed a 410, then I assume
that when they are out of politics they won't be required to file
a 415 Statement of Termination?

Thank you in advance for your speedy reply.

Sincerely,

Bob Mulholland

Bob Mulholland
BM:mr

sections.question: Specifically, you asked whether the Builders, Associates, Contractors Political Action Committee (BAC-PAC) must file an amended Statement of Organization to comply with the new requirements for identification of committee sponsors.
sections.conclusion: Because, as you have stated, the BAC-PAC is sponsored by the Building Industry Association of Central California, the PAC is required to amend its Statement of Organization to include the name of the Association in the committee's name. One suggestion would be to simply call it the Building Industry Association of Central California Political Action Committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a sponsored political action committee (BAC-PAC) sponsored by the Building Industry Association of Central California file an amended Statement of Organization to include the sponsor's name in the committee name to comply with new 1986 identification requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The BAC-PAC must amend its Statement of Organization to include the sponsor's name. The committee should use a name such as 'Building Industry Association of Central California Political Action Committee,' with allowable abbreviations, and must use this single name consistently across all required filings and communications.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated March 12, 1986) and FPPC response advice letter (dated April 9, 1986). Additional unrelated incoming request from Bob Mulholland (April 2, 1986) is appended but not relevant to FPPC A-86-110. Extraction focuses on the primary advice letter response. Minor handwriting/signature artifacts present but do not affect content extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Specifically, you asked whether the Builders, Associates, Contractors Political Action Committee (BAC-PAC) must file an amended Statement of Organization to comply with the new requirements for identification of committee sponsors.

CONCLUSION: Because, as you have stated, the BAC-PAC is sponsored by the Building Industry Association of Central California, the PAC is required to amend its Statement of Organization to include the name of the Association in the committee's name. One suggestion would be to simply call it the Building Industry Association of Central California Political Action Committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-86-110 addresses whether a sponsored political action committee must amend its Statement of Organization to comply with new 1986 requirements mandating that sponsored committees include their sponsor's name in their committee name. The FPPC advises that an amended Form 410 must be filed with the sponsor's name included and the new name must be used consistently across all campaign disclosures, mailings, and related filings.
